root_text.sn3.23:1.2: Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho rājā pasenadi kosalo bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn3.23:1.3: “kati nu kho, bhante, lokassa dhammā uppajjamānā uppajjanti ahitāya dukkhāya aphāsuvihārāyā”ti?
root_text.sn3.23:1.4: “Tayo kho, mahārāja, lokassa dhammā uppajjamānā uppajjanti ahitāya dukkhāya aphāsuvihārāya.
root_text.sn3.23:1.6: Lobho kho, mahārāja, lokassa dhammo, uppajjamāno uppajjati ahitāya dukkhāya aphāsuvihārāya.
root_text.sn3.23:1.7: Doso kho, mahārāja, lokassa dhammo, uppajjamāno uppajjati ahitāya dukkhāya aphāsuvihārāya.
root_text.sn3.23:1.8: Moho kho, mahārāja, lokassa dhammo, uppajjamāno uppajjati ahitāya dukkhāya aphāsuvihārāya.
root_text.sn3.23:1.9: Ime kho, mahārāja, tayo lokassa dhammā uppajjamānā uppajjanti ahitāya dukkhāya aphāsuvihārāyā”ti.

translation_text.sn3.23:1.2: Seated to one side, King Pasenadi said to the Buddha,
translation_text.sn3.23:1.3: “Sir, how many things arise in the world for its harm, suffering, and discomfort?”
translation_text.sn3.23:1.4: “Great king, three things arise in the world for its harm, suffering, and discomfort.
translation_text.sn3.23:1.9: These three things arise in the world for its harm, suffering, and discomfort.”
translation_text.sn3.23:1.10: That is what the Buddha said. …
translation_text.sn3.23:2.3: they harm a person of wicked heart,
translation_text.sn3.23:2.4: as a bamboo is destroyed by its own fruit.”

reference_text.sn3.23:1.1: bj13.180, dr15.134, ms12S1_743, msdiv134, pts-vp-pli1ed1.98, pts-vp-pli2ed1.219, sya15.143, vri23.117
